learnings.text: /zo/ask API ignores JSON output format requests in prompt templates. Returns natural language analysis regardless of output_format parameter. Need to parse semantic response instead of expecting structured JSON.

Security Gate processing time averages 15 seconds due to /zo/ask API calls. Acceptable for security-critical system but exceeds 5-second target. Consider async processing or caching for production deployment.
D2.1 is a manual drop type - deposits should use status 'blocked_human' until V confirms completion of manual steps. Do not mark complete just because documentation was created.

D1.3 deposit claimed complete but was missing the core zoputer_client.py script specified in the drop requirements. Created it during D2.1 execution. Future deposits should verify ALL artifacts listed in the drop spec exist before marking complete.
Pulse learnings CLI: use 'list <build_slug>' to read build learnings; there is no 'read' subcommand (docs/briefs may reference outdated command). Source: D4.1

When executing a Drop, extract build_slug and drop_id from the drop brief frontmatter and pass them to session_state_manager.py init via --build and --worker-num flags. Without these, the router cannot detect worker context and will misroute to thread-close instead of drop-close. Source: D4.1
bundle_manager.py (D1.3) created JSON bundles but daily export needs tarballs - updated transmit_bundle to handle both formats
Temp directories get cleaned up before async operations - use persistent bundle storage (N5/data/bundles/)
Git-based change detection works well for skills but requires tracking last_export.json state
